key_facts: The Rheonics SRV-X7-RP is a reactor probe designed for inline process density and viscosity monitoring.
The probe is constructed from 316L material.
It can withstand a maximum pressure of 500 bar (7500 psi).
The SRV-X7-RP is available in four standard lengths: 150mm, 250mm, 350mm, and 450mm, with a length tolerance of ±1 mm.
The product is protected by US and International patents, both granted and pending.
Rheonics has contact information for Switzerland and USA, including phone numbers and email address.
warnings_notes: Protected by US and International patents granted and pending.
This drawing remains property of rheonics. It may be neither copied nor informed to third persons or competitors without our approval. ( 2 paragraph 1 fig.7 copyright act of 9th Sept. 1965)

key_facts: The document outlines the order code structure for Rheonics Reactor Probe (RP) sensors, specifically the Type-SR -X7-RP model series.
Customers must specify the sensor type (viscometer or combined density and viscometer), the length variant, and the process connection type when ordering.
Sensor types available for selection include the Rheonics SRV viscometer and the Rheonics SRD density meter and viscometer.
Available length variants for the sensor probe are 150 mm, 250 mm, 350 mm, and 450 mm.
Connection types are compression fittings with a minimum outer diameter of 22 mm, made from either PTFE (P) or SS316L (S) material.
Two example configurations are provided: SRV-X7-RP250-B1NOS, which features a 1" NPT SS316L compression fitting, and SRD-X7-RP250-B34NS, which features a 3/4" NPT SS316L compression fitting.
Rheonics sensors are protected by granted and pending US and International patents.
Rheonics contact information includes addresses for Switzerland and USA, website, email, and phone numbers.
